title: Mutating Methods 🧬
description: How a Struct can change its own internal data safely.

slides.title: The Struct Security Lockdown 🔒
The Override Switch 🧬

quizData.question: If a Struct method tries to change the Struct's own property without the `mutating` keyword, what happens?

options.text: It creates a new copy of the property.
Swift throws an error and refuses to compile.

quizData.explanation: Structs are extremely safe by design. To prevent accidental data overrides, Swift forces you to explicitly authorize mutations.

slides.title: See It in Action
slides.content: Run this code and see how the mutating method successfully alters the internal `balance` of the BankAccount struct.
